name: Sweet orange (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh edible weight (~100 mL; concentration_mM computed treating 100 g as 0.1 L)

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 169097 (SR Legacy 09200), Oranges raw all commercial varieties

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: USDA per-sugar profile; corroborated by juice HPLC ratio ~2:1:1 sucrose:glucose:fructose (Frontiers 2023, PMC9858198)

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: USDA per-sugar profile; juice HPLC 22.06-25.12 g/L (PMC9858198)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Dominant sugar; USDA; juice HPLC 45.70-52.92 g/L (PMC9858198)

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Frontiers/PMC9858198 (Navel/Cara Cara/Kirkwood juice, HPLC 13.52-16.02 g/L); midpoint per 100 mL
components.evidence: Dominant organic acid of sweet orange (46-58% of total acids); MISSING from FooDB record

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Second organic acid; was present as L-Malate but unquantified

components.name: quinic acid
components.evidence: Third organic acid; MISSING from FooDB record

components.name: L-ascorbic acid (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Also an organic acid; L-Ascorbate present in record, now quantified

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: highest protein-bound AA; free asparagine also abundant in orange

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: USDA amino acid profile (as cystine dimer)

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: USDA amino acid profile; free proline is a major free-AA in citrus

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: USDA amino acid profile (L-serine; record's D-Serine is an artifact)

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: USDA amino acid profile (was absent from FooDB record)

components.name: L-asparagine
components.evidence: One of the dominant FREE amino acids in orange; present, not separately quantified by USDA (folded into aspartate)

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: USDA; was MISSING from FooDB record

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: provitamin-A activity from carotenoids only (no preformed retinol)

components.name: beta-cryptoxanthin
components.evidence: characteristic citrus carotenoid; dominant provitamin-A carotenoid here

components.name: dietary fiber (incl. pectin)
components.evidence: orange fiber is pectin-rich; largely non-fermentable by many models

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: major PUFA of the small lipid pool

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace element; FooDB predicted, not growth-relevant

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: cobalt ion ultratrace; not a defined growth nutrient of orange; FooDB noise

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: ultratrace non-essential-for-microbe element; FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic pharmaceutical estrogen; biologically implausible in a plant food; FooDB predicted

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial-fermentation food preservative; not native to orange

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: predicted allo-isoleucine stereoisomer artifact; real L-isoleucine already included

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed vitamin A is absent in plant foods (USDA retinol = 0); provitamin-A carotenoids are the real form

remove.name: Vitamin D
remove.reason: not present in citrus (plant food); FooDB predicted/implausible

missing_added: citric acid (1.48 g/100g) - dominant organic acid, absent from FooDB record
quinic acid (0.74 g/100g) - absent from record
succinic acid (0.27 g/100g) - absent from record
malic acid quantified (0.42 g/100g; was unquantified L-Malate)
individual sugars quantified: sucrose 4.28, glucose 2.24, fructose 2.53 g/100g
full amino-acid amounts incl. L-valine (was missing) and asparagine flag
keep_uncovered: coumarins/furanocoumarins: xanthyletin, xanthoxyletin, scoparone, nordentatin, oxypeucedanin, suberenol, angelical, citracridone I, citrusinine I/II
boron (genuine plant micronutrient, no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169097 (SR Legacy 09200) - Oranges, raw, all commercial varieties
Frontiers in Nutrition / PMC9858198 (2023) - Juices and By-Products of Red-Fleshed Sweet Oranges: sugars and organic acids by HPLC in Navel, Cara Cara, Kirkwood juice
HPLC organic-acid literature for orange juice (citric > quinic > malic > succinic)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources: sucrose > fructose ~ glucose (total ~9.3 g/100g) plus a large organic-acid pool dominated by citric acid (~1.5 g/100g) with quinic, malic and succinic acids - the organic acids were the main gap in the FooDB record and are a major fermentable carbon source. Nitrogen is thin (0.94 g protein/100g) with free asparagine, proline and arginine dominating the free-amino pool. Organic-acid amounts are taken from fresh/juice HPLC (edible orange is ~85% juice) and expressed per 100 g treated as ~100 mL; treat as good estimates (range noted per component). Removed toxic heavy metals, ultratrace non-nutritive elements, a synthetic estrogen and a stereoisomer artifact; retained genuine citrus phytochemicals as uncovered.
